channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: For a quarter century, President Vladimir Putin has faced the specter of Russia's shrinking and aging population. In 1999, a year before he came to power, the number of babies born in Russia plunged to its lowest recorded level.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa poised to become a hurricane and drop torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa is expected to strengthen into a hurricane, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: Japan's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi faces significant foreign policy challenges just days after taking office.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Inspector Clouseau? The mystery man in an AP photo after the Louvre jewel heist creates a buzz
item.description: PARIS (AP) — It was shortly after the stunning heist of the crown jewels at the Louvre when Paris-based Associated Press photographer Thibault Camus caught in his frame a dapperly dressed young man walking by uniformed French police officers, their car blocking one of the museum gates.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Asia return and East Timor’s entry take spotlight in landmark ASEAN summit
item.description: Southeast Asian foreign ministers have started talks ahead of a landmark ASEAN summit that will welcome East Timor as the bloc’s 11th member and mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar in vivid hat, long hidden from view, sells for 32 million euros
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A vividly hued Picasso portrait of longtime muse and partner Dora Maar that had remained hidden from public view for more than eight decades sold Friday at auction for 32 million euros (about $37 million), including fees — surpassing expectations but far from the artist’s most expensive
item.dc:creator: OLEG CETINIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio tours US-led center in Israel overseeing the ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: KIRYAT GAT, Israel (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday toured a U.S.-led center in Israel overseeing the Gaza ceasefire, as the Trump administration worked to set up an international security force in the territory and shore up the tenuous truce between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader vows to further boost defense spending as regional tensions rise
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, in her first major policy speech on Friday, pledged to further accelerate Japan’s military buildup and spending and complete an early upgrade of the country’s security strategy as tensions rise with China, North Korea and Russia.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Men sentenced over London arson attack masterminded by Russia as part of a wider sabotage campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A British judge on Friday sentenced five men for their roles in an arson attack in London that authorities say was masterminded by Russian intelligence as part of a wider campaign of sabotage and disruption. Their sentences ranged from seven to 17 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US deploys Ford carrier strike group to combat narco-terror in Western Hemisphere
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group is being deployed to continue targeting suspected drug smuggling boats in the Caribbean, the Pentagon said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rand Paul: Trump could see 'all hell break loose' with further involvement in Ukraine, Venezuela
item.description: Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) warned on Friday that President Trump's growing involvements in Ukraine's war with Russia and in the Caribbean Sea against Venezuela could further unravel and greatly upset the MAGA base.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: How a German freight lift became an unexpected social media star in the Louvre heist
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Forget France: The Louvre Museum jewelry heist was a classic case of German efficiency. Photos of a German-made freight lift that the thieves used in a lightning-fast daylight heist at the Louvre have gone viral. And its manufacturer is now riding high from the unexpected endorsement.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey in talks with Qatar and Oman to buy used Eurofighter jets, Erdogan says
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey is negotiating with Qatar and Oman to acquire used Eurofighter Typhoon fighter jets as part of its effort to bolster its air force capabilities, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said in comments released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International Criminal Court judges reject jurisdiction challenge in Duterte case
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — International Criminal Court judges on Thursday rejected a challenge to jurisdiction in the case of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, who is accused of involvement in dozens of killings as part of his so-called war on drugs when in office.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuanian president says Russian military planes violated the Baltic country's airspace
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Russian military planes briefly violated Lithuania’s airspace Thursday evening, the Lithuanian president said, condemning what he called a blatant breach of the territorial integrity of his European Union and NATO-member country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Krafton shifting to 'AI-first' game development
item.description: The makers of popular video game "PUBG: Battlegrounds" are switching gears and becoming an "AI-first" developer.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Putin deplores US sanctions as 'unfriendly' while EU joins in heaping restrictions on Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday heaped more economic sanctions on Russia, adding to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new punitive measures the previous day against the Russian oil industry.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bipartisan senators criticize Trump’s nominee for Kuwait over accusations of antisemitism
item.description: Republican and Democratic members of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee on Thursday demonstrated rare unity opposing President Trump’s nominee for ambassador to Kuwait, scrutinizing a history of alleged antisemitic comments and positions.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians attend rival rallies in Budapest as Orbán faces election test
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians filled the streets of Budapest on Thursday in competing demonstrations as supporters of the country’s two main political movements staged mutual shows of strength before next spring’s national election.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: Putin adviser says Trump chose 'path of war' with oil sanctions
item.description: Former Russian President Dmitry Medvedev said Thursday the country no longer would have to worry about “meaningless” peace negotiations with President Trump, a day after the Treasury Department announced sanctions against two Russian oil companies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US officials headed to Israel as violence tests ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: After a weekend of violence, Monday's discussions are expected to center on the second phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Thieves steal crown jewels in 4 minutes from Louvre Museum
item.description: PARIS (AP) — In a minutes-long strike Sunday inside the world’s most-visited museum, thieves rode a basket lift up the Louvre ’s facade, forced a window, smashed display cases and fled with priceless Napoleonic jewels, officials said.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
